input.old_string:
## Run Stopping Point

Halting here per (a) operator AFK, (b) Claude Code sensitive-path protection blocks further dark-factory progress without configuration changes, (c) findings file is rich enough to be valuable input to the dark-factory team. Tasks 1-5 in session task list reflect terminal state.
input.new_string:
## Run Stopping Point — superseded by manual implementation pass below

Halting here per (a) operator AFK, (b) Claude Code sensitive-path protection blocks further dark-factory progress without configuration changes, (c) findings file is rich enough to be valuable input to the dark-factory team. Tasks 1-5 in session task list reflect terminal state.

---

## Manual Implementation Pass (after operator returned)

Operator instruction: "keep going and making things happen ... I want all the plans implemented." Manual implementation pass on branch `2026/05/07-1955-caleb-feat-mobile-scanner-workflows` started after the dark-factory pipeline halted. Strategy: cherry-pick plan 03's worktree commits, then dispatch one general-purpose implementation subagent per plan, sequentially. Each subagent runs in the operator's main project directory (no `.claude/`-path issue, Finding #13 sidestepped).

### Manual fixes recorded as I went

**MF-1: Cherry-pick blocked by file lock on `vault/.chronicler/chronicler.db`.** `git cherry-pick 3300c53 e2e533b` failed with `Permission denied` on the chronicler DB. Workaround: `cp` the worktree files directly into the main project, then `git add` + commit. The two factory commits in the worktree were the source of truth for SS-01 + SS-02; SS-03's `useBarcodeScanner.ts` was uncommitted but recoverable from the worktree's working tree.

**MF-2: ScannerErrorKind redeclaration mismatch.** The factory worker's `useBarcodeScanner.ts` re-declared `ScannerErrorKind` locally with values that didn't match `web/src/types/scanner.ts`. Hook had `'permission-denied' | 'no-camera' | 'camera-in-use' | 'decode-error' | 'insecure-context' | 'unknown'`; types module had `'permission-denied' | 'no-camera' | 'camera-in-use' | 'decode-error' | 'unknown'`. Fix: extended types module's union to be the canonical set (added `insecure-context`, `unsupported`), exported `ScannerError` interface from there, removed hook's local re-declaration. One source of truth.

**MF-3: Default cooldown drift.** Worker defaulted `cooldownMs = 2000` in the hook signature; spec REQ-005 says 1000ms. Single-line edit.

**MF-4: docs/tests/ is gitignored.** Plan 03 SS-05 wanted to land scanner-fixtures.md under `docs/tests/`. Per project commit `656345a`, that path is intentionally gitignored. Decision: leave plan 03's fixtures at `docs/tests/scanner-fixtures.md` (gitignored, local-only) and move other plan-06 readiness docs to `docs/sale-day/` (tracked). Plan 06's smoke-test scenarios at `docs/tests/2026-05-07-sale-day-readiness/` are also gitignored — operator can `git add -f` if they want them tracked.

**MF-5: SaleClosed → SaleClosedBlocked enum value.** Plan 04 spec referenced `FulfillmentResultType.SaleClosed`; actual API enum is `SaleClosedBlocked`. Implementation subagent caught this and matched the real API.

**MF-6: Wrong-code-type tightening.** Plan 04 SS-03's strict `classifyMobileScanInput === 'order-number'` would flag any item barcode lacking the `PL-/PLB-/PLS-` prefix as "wrong code type" because `looksLikeOrderNumberLookup` is permissive. Subagent tightened to "order-shaped AND not the current order's number" so backend remains the integrity boundary for `WrongOrder`.

**MF-7: ScanResponse missing full lines[].** Plan 04 SS-03 wanted the progress bar to update after each scan, but `ScanResponse` only carries the changed line + `remainingItems`. To honor REQ-022 (no optimistic mutation), subagent calls `ordersApi.getById` after each Accepted scan to pull authoritative state. One extra request per accepted scan; matches spec's "single getById on mount + per-response refresh over polling" preference.

**MF-8: View Order navigation target undefined.** Plan 05 spec says LookupPrint role gets a ghost "View Order" CTA, but no read-only mobile order-detail route exists. Subagent rendered the ghost button as `disabled` (visible, labeled, non-actionable) so the spec contract holds visually. One-line wiring change if/when a target route is added.

**MF-9: Real audit findings discovered during plan 06 SS-03.** Plan 06's hardening checklist is read-only by design — failures get filed as backlog rather than fixed inline. Two real issues surfaced:
- **REGRESSION:LOW** — `MobileOrderLookupPage.tsx:185-189` has a comment saying "no console.log of code" but the `console.debug` payload includes `code: result.code`. (`console.debug` ≠ `console.log` so the literal claim is true, but the spirit is violated.) Filed as backlog.
- **BACKLOG:P1** — `FulfillmentEvent` model has no `UserId` / `Source` / `StationId` columns. Audit attribution today depends on the paper volunteer assignment sheet. Backend extension needed to make scan attribution machine-readable.

### Final state

| Plan | Spec | Implementation | Tests | Build |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 03 camera-scanner | 30/35 | ✅ All 5 sub-specs | 5 BarcodeScanner + 19 helpers + 3 mobileRouteConfig + scanner.types | ✅ |
| 04 mobile-pickup | 28/30 | ✅ All 6 sub-specs | 13 logic + 6 lookup-page + 10 scan-page = 29 | ✅ |
| 05 mobile-lookup | 28/30 | ✅ All 5 sub-specs | 11 selection + 13 page = 24 | ✅ |
| 06 readiness | 28/30 | ✅ All 4 sub-specs (docs only) | n/a (read-only audit) | n/a |

**Verification:** `npx vitest run` across all mobile + scanner test files: **93/93 pass**. `npm run build`: **clean (9s)**. `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json`: **clean**.

**Branch:** `2026/05/07-1955-caleb-feat-mobile-scanner-workflows` with 5 commits:
1. `b7cd433 feat(plan-03 SS-01/02/03): scanner types, helpers, useBarcodeScanner hook`
2. `e447dff feat(plan-03 SS-04/SS-05): BarcodeScanner component + demo route + docs`
3. `d0bf495 feat(plan-04): mobile pickup scan workflow (SS-01..SS-06)`
4. `1738035 feat(plan-05): mobile order lookup workflow (SS-01..SS-05)`
5. `(plan-06 commit hash) feat(plan-06): sale-day readiness + hardening docs (SS-01..SS-04)`

Operator can PR this branch to `main` when ready. Two backlog items to file separately (MF-9 above) and seven `<TBD>` placeholders in the readiness docs (sale-day endpoint URL, JWT config, admin seed tooling, print template path, inventory reconciliation, audit surface, FulfillmentEvent attribution).

### Comparison: Dark Factory autonomous vs. manual subagent dispatch

| Aspect | Dark Factory (autonomous) | Manual subagent dispatch |
|---|---|---|
| Setup cost | High — must clear path protection, rate limits, baseline-gate failures | Low — operator's main directory, no protection issues |
| Per sub-spec time | 3-10 min when working; crashes mid-flight on large sub-specs | 5-20 min per agent; covers 4-6 sub-specs each |
| Failure recovery | Silent exit-1; state.db lies about completion | Synchronous report from subagent with concrete deltas |
| Transparency | Heavy stdout logging but real failures buried | Subagent reports its own deltas + judgment calls |
| Adversarial review | spec-audit + spec-quality + coherence are real value-adds | None — relies on the spec being correct |
| Total turnaround for 20 sub-specs | Did not complete (crashed at 2/5 of one plan) | ~70 minutes wall-clock (4 plans, all sub-specs) |

For the dark-factory team: the spec-audit / spec-quality / coherence subsystem is genuinely the best part of this stack. If the worker dispatch + recovery layer can be hardened (Findings #3, #5, #10, #13), and worktrees moved off `~/.claude/`, this would beat manual subagent dispatch handily.

+**Verification:** `npx vitest run` across all mobile + scanner test files: **93/93 pass**. `npm run build`: **clean (9s)**. `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json`: **clean**.
+1. `b7cd433 feat(plan-03 SS-01/02/03): scanner types, helpers, useBarcodeScanner hook`
+2. `e447dff feat(plan-03 SS-04/SS-05): BarcodeScanner component + demo route + docs`
+5. `(plan-06 commit hash) feat(plan-06): sale-day readiness + hardening docs (SS-01..SS-04)`

+Operator can PR this branch to `main` when ready. Two backlog items to file separately (MF-9 above) and seven `<TBD>` placeholders in the readiness docs (sale-day endpoint URL, JWT config, admin seed tooling, print template path, inventory reconciliation, audit surface, FulfillmentEvent attribution).
+### Comparison: Dark Factory autonomous vs. manual subagent dispatch
+| Aspect | Dark Factory (autonomous) | Manual subagent dispatch |
+| Setup cost | High — must clear path protection, rate limits, baseline-gate failures | Low — operator's main directory, no protection issues |
+| Per sub-spec time | 3-10 min when working; crashes mid-flight on large sub-specs | 5-20 min per agent; covers 4-6 sub-specs each |
+| Failure recovery | Silent exit-1; state.db lies about completion | Synchronous report from subagent with concrete deltas |
+| Transparency | Heavy stdout logging but real failures buried | Subagent reports its own deltas + judgment calls |
+| Adversarial review | spec-audit + spec-quality + coherence are real value-adds | None — relies on the spec being correct |
+| Total turnaround for 20 sub-specs | Did not complete (crashed at 2/5 of one plan) | ~70 minutes wall-clock (4 plans, all sub-specs) |

+For the dark-factory team: the spec-audit / spec-quality / coherence subsystem is genuinely the best part of this stack. If the worker dispatch + recovery layer can be hardened (Findings #3, #5, #10, #13), and worktrees moved off `~/.claude/`, this would beat manual subagent dispatch handily.

content.text:
Honestly: **the code is green, but "ready for me" depends on what you mean.**

**Green ✅:**
- 93/93 tests for the new mobile/scanner code pass
- TypeScript: clean
- `npm run build`: succeeds (9s)
- All 4 plans committed on `2026/05/07-1955-caleb-feat-mobile-scanner-workflows`

**Not yet done ⚠️:**

1. **Branch isn't merged to main.** Still ahead 5 commits — needs your PR + review.

2. **No physical-device testing yet.** Every spec had `[HUMAN REVIEW]` criteria for "actually open `/mobile/scanner-demo` on Android Chrome + iPhone Safari, scan a real QR + a real 1D barcode, verify camera permission flow / torch / duplicate cooldown / tab-switch cleanup." That's the only way to confirm camera scanning actually *works* in the real world. Tests use a mocked `useBarcodeScanner`. The hook touches real `BrowserMultiFormatReader` / `getUserMedia` / track lifecycle — none of which a vitest+jsdom setup can exercise.

3. **HTTPS gate.** Camera scanning requires `window.isSecureContext` (HTTPS or localhost). The hook surfaces a friendly `insecure-context` error if you try to use it over plain HTTP. So plugging your phone into a dev box on a LAN won't work without an HTTPS reverse proxy or `localhost` tunneling.

4. **Pre-existing test failures unrelated to this work** — there are ~23 failing test files in the broader suite (use `node:test` instead of vitest) plus 3 pre-existing JoyAriaLive flakes. Confirmed by the implementation subagent that they predate this branch.

5. **Two backlog items found during plan 06 audit:**
   - `MobileOrderLookupPage` `console.debug` payload includes the scan code (low severity).
   - `FulfillmentEvent` model has no `UserId`/`Source`/`StationId` — audit attribution still depends on a paper sheet.

6. **7 `<TBD>` placeholders in plan 06 readiness docs** need decisions before sale day (sale-day endpoint URL, JWT config, admin seed tooling, print template path, inventory reconciliation, audit surface, FulfillmentEvent attribution).

**Recommended next step:** spin up `cd web && npm run dev`, install the PWA on your phone, hit `/mobile/scanner-demo`, and run the device matrix at `docs/sale-day/morning-of-checklist.md`. That's where you'll find anything the autonomous tests can't surface.

file.content:
@echo off
setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

:: Change to the directory where this script lives
cd /d "%~dp0"

:: Check for admin rights (needed for firewall rules)
net session >nul 2>&1
if %errorlevel% neq 0 (
    echo [!] Not running as Administrator.
    echo [!] Right-click start.bat and "Run as administrator" for LAN access.
    echo [!] Continuing without firewall setup -- localhost-only.
    echo.
    goto :start_docker
)

:: --- Firewall Rules ---
echo [*] Configuring Windows Firewall...

netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="HamptonHawks-Web" >nul 2>&1
netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="HamptonHawks-API" >nul 2>&1

netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="HamptonHawks-Web" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=3000 profile=private,domain >nul
netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="HamptonHawks-API" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=8080 profile=private,domain >nul

echo [OK] Firewall rules added for ports 3000 and 8080.
echo.

:start_docker
:: --- Get host IP (skip link-local 169.254.x.x and loopback) ---
set "HOST_IP="
for /f "tokens=2 delims=:" %%a in ('ipconfig ^| findstr /c:"IPv4 Address" ^| findstr /v "WSL vEthernet"') do (
    if not defined HOST_IP (
        set "RAW=%%a"
        for /f "tokens=*" %%b in ("!RAW!") do set "CANDIDATE=%%b"
        echo !CANDIDATE! | findstr /b "169.254. 127." >nul || set "HOST_IP=!CANDIDATE!"
    )
)

echo ============================================
echo   Hampton Hawks Plant Sales
echo ============================================
echo.
echo   Local:
echo     Web:      http://localhost:3000
echo     Swagger:  http://localhost:8080/swagger
echo.
if not defined HOST_IP goto :skip_lan
echo   LAN (other devices on your network^):
echo     Web:      http://!HOST_IP!:3000
echo     Swagger:  http://!HOST_IP!:8080/swagger
echo.
:skip_lan
echo ============================================
echo.

docker-compose up -d --build

echo.
echo Done!
pause

content.content:
services:
  postgres:
    ports: !override
      - "127.0.0.1:5433:5432"
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    container_name: hamptonhawks-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-plantapp}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-change-me}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-hampton_hawks_plant_sales}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-plantapp} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-hampton_hawks_plant_sales}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

  app:
    build:
      context: ./api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: hamptonhawks-app
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      ASPNETCORE_URLS: http://+:8080
      ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT: Production
      ConnectionStrings__Default: Host=db;Port=5432;Database=${POSTGRES_DB:-hampton_hawks_plant_sales};Username=${POSTGRES_USER:-plantapp};Password=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-change-me}
      APP_ADMIN_PIN: ${APP_ADMIN_PIN:-1234}
      INVENTORY_NEGATIVE_ADJUST_THRESHOLD: ${INVENTORY_NEGATIVE_ADJUST_THRESHOLD:-10}
      Cors__AllowedOrigins: ${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:-http://localhost:3000}
    ports:
      - "${APP_PORT:-8080}:8080"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

volumes:
  postgres_data:
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: plantapp
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: plantapp
      POSTGRES_DB: hampton_hawks_plant_sales
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U plantapp -d hampton_hawks_plant_sales"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

  api:
    build: ./api
    environment:
      ASPNETCORE_URLS: http://+:8080
      ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT: Production
      ConnectionStrings__Default: Host=postgres;Port=5432;Database=hampton_hawks_plant_sales;Username=plantapp;Password=plantapp
      APP_ADMIN_PIN: "1234"
      INVENTORY_NEGATIVE_ADJUST_THRESHOLD: "10"
      Bootstrap__AdminUsername: "admin"
      Bootstrap__AdminPassword: "changeme"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "0.0.0.0:8080:8080"
    healthcheck:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
@echo off
setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

:: Change to the directory where this script lives
cd /d "%~dp0"

:: Check for admin rights (needed for firewall rules)
net session >nul 2>&1
if %errorlevel% neq 0 (
    echo [!] Not running as Administrator.
    echo [!] Right-click start.bat and "Run as administrator" for LAN access.
    echo [!] Continuing without firewall setup -- localhost-only.
    echo.
    goto :start_docker
)

:: --- Firewall Rules ---
echo [*] Configuring Windows Firewall...

netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="HamptonHawks-Web" >nul 2>&1
netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="HamptonHawks-API" >nul 2>&1

netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="HamptonHawks-Web" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=3000 profile=private,domain >nul
netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="HamptonHawks-API" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=8080 profile=private,domain >nul

echo [OK] Firewall rules added for ports 3000 and 8080.
echo.

:start_docker
:: --- Get host IP (skip link-local 169.254.x.x and loopback) ---
set "HOST_IP="
for /f "tokens=2 delims=:" %%a in ('ipconfig ^| findstr /c:"IPv4 Address" ^| findstr /v "WSL vEthernet"') do (
    if not defined HOST_IP (
        set "RAW=%%a"
        for /f "tokens=*" %%b in ("!RAW!") do set "CANDIDATE=%%b"
        echo !CANDIDATE! | findstr /b "169.254. 127." >nul || set "HOST_IP=!CANDIDATE!"
    )
)

echo ============================================
echo   Hampton Hawks Plant Sales
echo ============================================
echo.
echo   Local:
echo     Web:      http://localhost:3000
echo     Swagger:  http://localhost:8080/swagger
echo.
if not defined HOST_IP goto :skip_lan
echo   LAN (other devices on your network^):
echo     Web:      http://!HOST_IP!:3000
echo     Swagger:  http://!HOST_IP!:8080/swagger
echo.
:skip_lan
echo ============================================
echo.

docker-compose up -d --build

echo.
echo Done!
pause

content.text:
Done. Everything is on `main` and pushed.

**Try it on your phone:**
1. Run `start.bat` (as admin for LAN access)
2. On desktop, open `http://localhost:3000/connect-mobile` — you'll see a QR code, the LAN URL, and an HTTPS warning panel
3. Scan the QR with your phone's camera (point camera at QR → tap the link banner)
4. The phone opens `http://<your-LAN-IP>:3000/mobile`
5. **Manual entry will work over plain HTTP**; **camera scanning needs HTTPS** — the connect page lists 3 options (caddy reverse-proxy, cloudflared, ngrok) for getting HTTPS quickly. Paste the resulting `https://` URL into the URL field on the connect page and the QR re-renders for that.

**What landed on main (`e9373ff`):**
- Plan 02 mobile joy shell + PWA (was already pending)
- Plans 03/04/05/06 fully implemented (93 new tests, all green)
- `/connect-mobile` desktop QR pairing page with HTTPS guidance
- `start.bat` now prints `/mobile` and `/connect-mobile` URLs (local + LAN) and the HTTPS caveat
- Branch `2026/05/07-1955-caleb-feat-mobile-scanner-workflows` is also pushed if you want it for any post-merge reference

**For real camera testing on phone**, fastest path: `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:3000` → paste the generated `https://...trycloudflare.com` URL into the connect page → scan the regenerated QR.

content: Goal was to dogfood the dark factory across 4 mobile plans and ship the implementations; all four plans are now manually implemented, merged to main, and pushed. Next: open `/connect-mobile` on desktop and scan the QR from your phone to test the mobile app. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text: The Dockerfile fails because `package.json` has a `postinstall` script (`node scripts/generate-pwa-icons.mjs` from plan 02) but the Dockerfile copies `package.json` first and runs `npm install` BEFORE copying `scripts/`. Pre-existing bug exposed by the docker rebuild. Fixing the Dockerfile.

file.content:
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app

COPY package.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
RUN rm -rf node_modules/.cache
RUN npm run build

# Stage 2: Serve with nginx
FROM nginx:alpine AS runtime
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

input.new_string:
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package manifests + lockfile + scripts referenced by lifecycle hooks.
# The package.json `postinstall` script runs scripts/generate-pwa-icons.mjs,
# so scripts/ must be present before `npm install` (or install must skip
# lifecycle scripts).
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
COPY scripts ./scripts
RUN npm install
COPY . .
RUN rm -rf node_modules/.cache
RUN npm run build

file.content:
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: plantapp
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: plantapp
      POSTGRES_DB: hampton_hawks_plant_sales
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U plantapp -d hampton_hawks_plant_sales"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

  api:
    build: ./api
    environment:
      ASPNETCORE_URLS: http://+:8080
      ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT: Production
      ConnectionStrings__Default: Host=postgres;Port=5432;Database=hampton_hawks_plant_sales;Username=plantapp;Password=plantapp
      APP_ADMIN_PIN: "1234"
      INVENTORY_NEGATIVE_ADJUST_THRESHOLD: "10"
      Bootstrap__AdminUsername: "admin"
      Bootstrap__AdminPassword: "changeme"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "0.0.0.0:8080:8080"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

  web:
    build: ./web
    depends_on:
      - api
    ports:
      - "0.0.0.0:3000:80"

volumes:
  postgres_data:

input.command:
cd .. && git add api/src/HamptonHawksPlantSales.Api/Program.cs 2>&1 && git commit -m "fix(api): register IPasswordHasher + AuthBootstrapService in DI

Login was failing 400 with 'Unable to resolve service for type IPasswordHasher
while attempting to activate UserService' because:
  1. IPasswordHasher had no DI registration despite being injected by
     UserService and AuthBootstrapService
  2. AuthBootstrapService (the IHostedService that seeds the bootstrap
     admin from Bootstrap__AdminUsername / Bootstrap__AdminPassword env
     vars) was not registered

Both now registered. Verified end-to-end: docker compose up + curl
/api/auth/login with admin/changeme returns 200 with the user payload,
and the AuthBootstrapService logs 'Bootstrap admin account admin
created.' on first boot.

Found by Playwright smoke test that walks login -> dashboard ->
connect-mobile -> /mobile/{lookup,pickup,scanner-demo}." 2>&1 | tail -5; git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
**Pushed to main as `5fe13a6`.**

Quick summary of what was fixed in this session beyond the original ask:

| Issue | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Bad Gateway | API container crashed on first boot — postgres wasn't ready in time | Manual `docker compose up -d api` brought it back |
| Login returned 400 with weird DI error | `IPasswordHasher` not registered in DI | Added `AddScoped<IPasswordHasher, PasswordHasher>()` |
| "Invalid credentials" with admin/changeme | `AuthBootstrapService` not registered as `IHostedService` — admin never seeded | Added `AddHostedService<AuthBootstrapService>()` |
| Smoke test passing | Playwright walks login → dashboard → connect-mobile → mobile routes | All green |

**Login is now `admin` / `changeme`** and the smoke test confirms every page renders. Re-run `start.bat` (or just `docker compose up -d --build api` if it's already up) to pick up the DI fix.

content.text:
**Confirmed in sync.** `origin/main` and local `HEAD` both at `96ed5de` — `feat(mobile): add /mobile/account page`.

The earlier hangs were stale `git.exe` processes holding the wincred credential helper from interrupted pushes; killing them cleared the lock.

**Final state on `main`:**
- `96ed5de` — mobile account page
- `5fe13a6` — DI fix (IPasswordHasher + AuthBootstrapService)
- `0c2ecae` — Dockerfile fix
- `e9373ff` — connect-mobile QR page
- `d268a85` — plan-06 readiness docs
- `1738035` — plan-05 mobile lookup
- `d0bf495` — plan-04 mobile pickup
- `e447dff` + `b7cd433` — plan-03 scanner

Re-run `start.bat` (force a rebuild on the web container with `docker compose up -d --build web` if it doesn't pick up automatically) and `/mobile/account` will load for the admin.
